I need help generating text prompts for AI text-to-images called Midjourneys. Can you create good text promps based on the ideas I have?
Here is information about the Midjourney:

About Midjourneys:
Midjourneys is an AI Text-to-image generator that was released in 2022. As the brands website states, its aim is to “explore new medium of thought and expand the imaginative powers in the human species”. Midjourneys asks you to enter a worded description for an image for example “a fox wearing top hat in a Roald Dahll illustration” and in a matter of seconds, you will be returned multiple images that match the description in the form a 4*4 image grid (or just a single image). These models have taught the relationship between an image as well as the text that describes it.
As of now, its 5th iteration (V4). V4 offers higher image quailty, more diverse output, wider stylistical range, support seamless textures, better image promotion, and dynamic rang.
Midjourneys V4 Prompt Guide:
In order to use the V4 model of Midjourney, add the –v 4 parameter to the prompt. This Model has very high coherency, it excels at interpeting natural language prompts and is high resolution. It supports advance features like –chaos and aspect ratios
In V4, to generate somethin other than a photograph, you will have to reference art movement, artistic techniques and genres, media types, games titles etc. In order to invoke the aesthetic of a specific image, you can be reference two or more things that will invoke the style you want.
- Art movement- Identifying the specific art movement in your prompt will introduce it’s specific style and techniques, examples include Impressionsm, Surrealsm, or Pop art
- Media type- Identifying what medium the image will be created in will determine its aesthetics, examples include photgraph, illustration, comics, concept art etc.
- Miedia title- Identifying a specific media influence will invoke its look. You can do this by referencing a media title, from the wizard of Oz or the game “Civilization” or from a specific artist. 
- Technique- Referencing techniques in your prompt can add that specific style to the generated image, examples include watercolor, pencil sketch or digital art
- Time period- Identifying which historical context will invoke its aesthetics. You can identify the time period by referencing the era in your prompt, for example images from the Rennaisance, Baroque or Modernist period.
- Geographic Location- Referencing regions or countries will influence the style, you can do this in your prompt by referencing the specific region or country you want the style of. For example you can reference Japanese Ukiyoe prints, African Tribal Art or American Abstract expressionism
Aspect Ratios
The –aspect or –ar parameter changes aspect ratio of generated images. An aspect raio is the width to height ratio of an images, typically expressed as 2 numbers separated by colons, such as “7:4” or “4:3”. The default aspect raio in Midjourneys is 1 : 1
–aspect must use only whole numbers, use “139:100” instead of “1.39 : 1”
The aspect raio impacts the shape/composition of the images that are generated
To use aspect ratio, add “–aspect <value> : <value>” or “–ar <value> <value>“ to the end your prompt
Chaoss
The –chaos or –c parameter influences the variatity of the images generated. High –chaos values produce more unusual, unexpected results and composites. Lower –chaos value will generate more reliable and repeatable results
–chaos accepts value from 0 to 100
The default –chaos is 0, so if you dont want too wild of a response you can use this
To use the chaos parameter add “–chaos <vaue>” or  “–c <value”> to the prompt
Higher –chaoss will help your grid have increasingly different, surprising styles in eacg square, as it if you asked multiple artists to give your promp a try. if you want fewer surpries styles/poses/ models/details in your grids set –chaos  to 0 and specify in your prompt what you want from Midjournies so it knows to avoid making its own surprises
Stylize 
Midjourney was trained to produce image that favor artistic colour, composition and forms. Th “–stylize” or “-s” parameter influences how strong this training is used. Low stylisation values will generate images that closely follow the prompt but lack the artistic touch. High stylisation values create images very much in the artistic realm but they are less connected to your prompt
-s can have values from 0 – 1000

## How to Use

1. Press `ALT+F11` to show the VBA Editor.

3. You can rename `"Matches"` at the top to your preferred output sheet name.
